intro.title: Prediction vs Reality
intro.description: Compare your anxious predictions with what actually happens to build a more balanced perspective.

complete.title: Activity Complete
complete.message: By looking at the facts, you're helping your mind learn that things are often more manageable than they seem.

comparison_options: It turned out almost exactly how I expected
Some parts were right, but not everything
It was quite different from what I expected
It was much easier/better than I imagined

s1.title: Think of a recent situation
s1.desc: Recall a recent moment where you felt anxious or unsure.

s2.title: What did you think would happen?
s2.desc: What were you expecting or fearing in that moment?

s3.title: How did you feel?
s3.desc: Select the emotions you felt and their intensity.

s4.title: What actually happened?
s4.desc: Focus on the facts of what really took place.

s6.title: What do you notice?
s6.desc: Was it as bad as you thought it would be?

s7.title: Next time
s7.desc: What could you remind yourself if this happens again?
s7.placeholder: e.g., "Maybe it won't be as bad as I'm imagining."

s8.title: A small reminder
s8.desc: Your mind tries to prepare you—but it doesn't always predict accurately. Every time you pause to look at the facts, you build resilience.
